steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: In 'of a good height, as could be seen even from the seated figure,' the word good modifies a physical dimension. We need the meaning of good that fits describing height as something notable or substantial.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Well-behaved: describes conduct, not physical size. ✗
  • Choice B — Considerable: means notable or substantial in amount—exactly what 'a good height' conveys when describing stature. ✓
  • Choice C — Capable: describes ability or skill, not a physical dimension. ✗
  • Choice D — Reliable: describes dependability, not size. ✗
That is the correct answer.
